title: asperula aristata var. pirinica
intro: Asperula aristata var. pirinica, also known as the Pirin milkwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and support the immune system. Asperula aristata var. pirinica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, and it is also used topically in the form of creams or salves to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
